_note: PROTOTYPE DATA. Federal benefit content in this file was verified against official sources (VA.gov, DOL, SBA, veteranscrisisline.net, NASDVA) on the lastVerified date shown for each category (2026-07-08 / 2026-07-09). Rules change — always confirm current details at the official source linked on each card. VetPath is not the VA and does not determine eligibility.

categories.name: VA disability compensation
categories.summary: Tax-free monthly compensation for conditions connected to your service.
categories.whoFor: Veterans with a current condition linked to an injury, illness, or event during service.
categories.sampleEligibility: Verified: you may qualify if you have a current diagnosed condition, an in-service injury, illness, or event, and a medical link between them — and you were discharged under other than dishonorable conditions.
actionSteps: Gather service records, medical records, and a symptom timeline.
If you're still serving with 180 to 90 days left before separation, ask about a Benefits Delivery at Discharge (BDD) pre-discharge claim.
Work with a free accredited VSO or county veteran service officer.
File your claim online at VA.gov or with VSO help.

categories.name: VA health care
categories.summary: Medical care, mental health, and prescriptions through the VA health system.
categories.whoFor: Most veterans who served in active duty and meet basic discharge and service requirements.
categories.sampleEligibility: Verified: most veterans who served on active duty and were discharged under other than dishonorable conditions can apply; your priority group depends on service history, disability rating, income, and toxic-exposure status (PACT Act).
actionSteps: Apply for VA health care online at VA.gov, by phone at 877-222-8387, or with a VSO.
Check PACT Act toxic-exposure eligibility — many veterans can now enroll directly without first applying for other VA benefits.
Choose a VA facility and schedule an intake appointment.
Ask about mental health and telehealth options.

categories.name: GI Bill & education
categories.summary: Tuition, housing allowance, and support for degrees, trades, and certifications.
categories.whoFor: Veterans and eligible dependents pursuing education or training.
categories.sampleEligibility: Verified: Post-9/11 GI Bill benefits scale with qualifying active-duty time — from 50% at 90 days up to 100% at 36 months. Transfers to a spouse or child must be requested and approved through DoD while you're still serving; they cannot be started after separation.
actionSteps: Apply for benefits and request a Certificate of Eligibility on VA.gov.
If transferring to dependents, request the transfer through DoD while still serving (generally 6 years in with a 4-year additional commitment).
Pick a program and confirm it's VA-approved using the GI Bill Comparison Tool.
Meet the School Certifying Official to enroll.

categories.name: VA home loan
categories.summary: A home loan guaranty with no down payment and no private mortgage insurance.
categories.whoFor: Veterans, service members, and some surviving spouses buying or refinancing a home.
categories.sampleEligibility: Verified: you'll need a Certificate of Eligibility based on your service history plus a lender's credit and income approval; a funding fee usually applies unless you're exempt, such as veterans receiving VA disability compensation.
actionSteps: Request your Certificate of Eligibility online at VA.gov, through your lender, or by mail (VA Form 26-1880).
Get pre-approved with a VA-experienced lender.
Shop with a real estate agent who knows VA appraisals and the VA loan process.

categories.name: VR&E (Chapter 31)
categories.summary: Veteran Readiness and Employment — training, education, and job support tied to a service-connected condition.
categories.whoFor: Veterans with a service-connected disability that affects their ability to work.
categories.sampleEligibility: Verified: you may qualify with a VA service-connected disability rating of at least 10%, a discharge that wasn't dishonorable, and an employment handicap; a Vocational Rehabilitation Counselor confirms entitlement. No time limit applies if you were discharged on or after January 1, 2013.
actionSteps: Apply for VR&E online at VA.gov (VA Form 28-1900).
Meet with a Vocational Rehabilitation Counselor for an initial evaluation.
Build an approved rehabilitation and employment plan.

categories.name: Employment & apprenticeships
categories.summary: Job placement, apprenticeships, and hiring programs for veterans.
categories.whoFor: Veterans and transitioning service members seeking work or a career change.
categories.sampleEligibility: Verified: veterans and eligible spouses receive priority of service for DOL-funded job training and placement programs at American Job Centers nationwide, with specialized help for those with significant barriers to employment.
actionSteps: Visit or register with your local American Job Center.
Ask about priority of service and JVSG-funded veteran employment specialists (DVOP/LVER staff).
Target veteran-friendly employers, apprenticeships, and registered apprenticeship programs.

categories.name: Veteran-owned business
categories.summary: SBA resources, training, and certifications for veteran entrepreneurs.
categories.whoFor: Veterans and military spouses starting or growing a business.
categories.sampleEligibility: Verified: SBA programs like Boots to Business and Veterans Business Outreach Centers serve the military community broadly; SDVOSB/VOSB certification requires a business at least 51% owned and controlled by veterans (service-disabled veterans for SDVOSB), certified through SBA.
actionSteps: Take the SBA Boots to Business course (offered through the DoD Transition Assistance Program).
Connect with your local Veterans Business Outreach Center (VBOC).
Apply for SDVOSB/VOSB certification through SBA's Veteran Small Business Certification (VetCert) program at veterans.certify.sba.gov.

categories.name: Mental health & crisis support
categories.summary: Confidential mental health care and 24/7 crisis support for veterans and families.
categories.whoFor: Any veteran, service member, or family member — you do not need to be enrolled in VA health care to use the Veterans Crisis Line.
categories.sampleEligibility: Verified: the Veterans Crisis Line is free, confidential, and available 24/7 to veterans, service members, and their families — no VA enrollment required. Dial 988 then press 1, text 838255, or chat online.

official.name: Veterans Crisis Line — dial 988 then press 1

actionSteps: Save the Veterans Crisis Line: dial 988, then press 1 — or text 838255, or chat online at veteranscrisisline.net.
Ask your VA facility about same-day mental health support.
Connect with a peer support group or Vet Center.

categories.name: Retirement & survivor benefits
categories.summary: Retirement pay coordination, pension, survivor protection, and family security.
categories.whoFor: Retiring veterans and their families planning for the long term.
categories.sampleEligibility: Verified: military retired pay (DFAS), VA Veterans Pension, and the Survivor Benefit Plan are separate programs with distinct rules — VA pension is needs-based for wartime veterans who meet age or disability plus income and net-worth limits.
actionSteps: Review retirement pay and Survivor Benefit Plan (SBP) elections with DFAS.
Check VA Veterans Pension and Aid & Attendance / Housebound eligibility at VA.gov.
Update beneficiaries and estate documents.

categories.name: Family & dependent benefits
categories.summary: Education, health, and support benefits for spouses and dependents.
categories.whoFor: Spouses, children, and dependents of veterans.
categories.sampleEligibility: Verified: spouses, dependent children, survivors, and caregivers may qualify for distinct programs — transferred GI Bill (DoD approval while still serving), DEA/Chapter 35 (veteran permanently and totally disabled or died from a service-connected condition), CHAMPVA (veteran rated permanently and totally disabled; not for TRICARE-eligible spouses), and VA caregiver support — each with its own rules.
actionSteps: Check whether GI Bill benefits can transfer to a dependent — the service member must request the transfer through DoD while still serving; it cannot be initiated after separation.

Explore DEA (Chapter 35) education benefits if the veteran is permanently and totally disabled from a service-connected condition, or died, is captured, or is missing — up to 36 months of benefits for those starting on or after August 1, 2018.
Check CHAMPVA health coverage for spouses and children of veterans rated permanently and totally disabled from a service-connected disability (not available if you qualify for TRICARE).

Look into VA caregiver support: the Program of Comprehensive Assistance for Family Caregivers (veteran rated 70% or higher; apply jointly with VA Form 10-10CG) or the Program of General Caregiver Support Services.

categories.name: State veteran benefits
categories.summary: State-specific benefits: property tax relief, tuition, licenses, parks, and more.
categories.whoFor: Veterans, varying by state of residence.
categories.sampleEligibility: Verified: every state and territory runs its own veterans agency with its own benefits — property tax relief, tuition programs, licenses, and recreation perks vary widely by state and change often, so eligibility must be confirmed directly with your state Department of Veterans Affairs.
actionSteps: Find your state Department of Veterans Affairs using the NASDVA state-by-state directory at nasdva.us/resources/ or VA's list of state veterans affairs offices on discover.va.gov.
Contact your state veterans agency or a county veteran service officer (county-level officers exist in many, but not all, states).
Ask specifically about property tax exemptions, state tuition programs, and license/registration benefits, and confirm current rules — state benefits change frequently.
